Higman 1964

Finite permutation groups of rank 3

Math. Zeitschr. 86 (1964), 145–156. DOI: 10.1007/BF01111335.

§1 Lemma 1 — paired orbits

0 formalized · 1 pending · 0 out of scope · total 1

Higman 1964, Lemma 1 (§1, Paired orbits) — 0/1 formalized, 1 pending .lean
  • Lemma 1 (paired orbit ⇔ even order). — A transitive permutation group `G` on `Ω` has a self-paired nontrivial `G_a`-orbit iff `|G|` is even Pending deferred L28

0 formalized · 1 pending · 0 out of scope · total 1

  • Lemma 2 (intersection numbers `λ, μ`). Pending deferred L33

§2 Lemma 3 — self-paired

0 formalized · 2 pending · 0 out of scope · total 2

  • Lemma 3 (self-paired structure under parity). Pending deferred L28
  • Corollary to Lemma 3 — (symmetry of `Δ` under even order) Pending deferred L31

§2 Lemma 4 — imprimitivity

0 formalized · 2 pending · 0 out of scope · total 2

  • Lemma 4 (imprimitivity criterion). Pending deferred L31
  • Corollary (rank-3 of odd order is primitive). Pending deferred L34

§3 Lemma 5 — block-design count

0 formalized · 3 pending · 0 out of scope · total 3

Higman 1964, Lemma 5 (§3, Block-design count) — 0/3 formalized, 3 pending .lean
  • Lemma 5 (μl = k(k − λ − 1)). Pending deferred L43
  • Corollary 2 — (`μ = 0 ⇔ G primitive and k ≤ l ⇔ λ = k − 1`) Pending deferred L46
  • Corollary 3 — (`G primitive ⇔ μ ∉ {0, k}`) Pending deferred L49

§4 Lemma 6 — two eigenvalues

0 formalized · 1 pending · 0 out of scope · total 1

Higman 1964, Lemma 6 (§4, Two eigenvalues `s, t`) — 0/1 formalized, 1 pending .lean
  • Lemma 6 (two eigenvalues `s, t`). — In addition to the eigenvalue `k` (multiplicity 1), the incidence matrix `A` of a rank-3 block design has exactly two further eigenvalues `s, t`, the roots of `X² − (λ − μ)X − (k − μ) = 0` (when `|G|` is even) or `X²… Pending deferred L42

  • Lemma 7 (Case I / Case II structure for even `|G|`). Pending deferred L31

§6 Theorem 1 — degree k²+1

2 formalized · 1 pending · 0 out of scope · total 3

Higman 1964, Theorem 1 (§6, Degree `k² + 1`) — 2/3 formalized, 1 pending .lean
  • Theorem 1 (Higman §6, full statement). — A transitive rank-3 permutation group of degree `n = k² + 1` (where `k` is the length of a `G_a`-orbit) has `n ∈ {5, 10, 50, 3250}`, equivalently `k ∈ {2, 3, 7, 57}`. The arithmetic core (Case II) is proven in `theore… Pending deferred L84
  • Theorem 1, Moore57 instance. — The Moore57 vertex count `n = 3250 = 57² + 1` is one of the four degrees in Higman's classification Formalized auto L90
  • Theorem 1, Moore57 valence instance. — k = 57` is one of the four valences `{2, 3, 7, 57}` allowed by Theorem 1 Formalized auto L97
Source & Lean proofs: github.com/yawara/moore57. Maintained by Yawara Ishida.
